title: Algebra Basics Quiz
subject: Mathematics
description: Test your knowledge of variables, equations, and basic algebra concepts.

questions.question: What is the value of x in 2x + 4 = 10?
questions.explanation: Subtract 4 from both sides: 2x = 6. Divide both sides by 2: x = 3.

questions.question: Which of the following is the quadratic formula?
questions.explanation: The quadratic formula is x = (-b ± √(b²-4ac)) / 2a, used to solve ax² + bx + c = 0.

questions.question: What is aᵐ × aⁿ equal to?
questions.explanation: When multiplying powers with the same base, add the exponents: aᵐ × aⁿ = a^(m+n).

questions.question: What is the slope in y = 3x + 7?
questions.explanation: In y = mx + b, m is the slope. Here m = 3.

questions.question: What is a² - b² factored?
questions.explanation: This is the difference of squares: a² - b² = (a+b)(a-b).

title: Cell Biology Quiz
subject: Science
description: Test your understanding of cell structure, organelles, and cell division.

questions.question: Which organelle is known as the 'powerhouse of the cell'?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria produce ATP (energy) through cellular respiration, earning the nickname 'powerhouse of the cell'.

questions.question: What is the key difference between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells?
questions.explanation: Eukaryotic cells have a membrane-bound nucleus containing their DNA. Prokaryotes lack this.

questions.question: Which process produces 4 genetically diverse gametes?
questions.explanation: Meiosis is cell division for sexual reproduction, producing 4 genetically unique gametes (sperm or eggs).

questions.question: What does the Golgi Apparatus do?
questions.explanation: The Golgi Apparatus processes, packages, and distributes proteins and lipids throughout and outside the cell.

questions.question: Which organelle is responsible for protein synthesis?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are the site of protein synthesis. They read mRNA and assemble amino acids into proteins.

title: World War II Quiz
subject: History
description: Test your knowledge of the causes, events, and consequences of World War II.

questions.question: What event triggered the start of World War II?
options: The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand
questions.explanation: Germany's invasion of Poland on September 1, 1939 prompted Britain and France to declare war on Germany, starting WWII.

questions.question: Which battle is considered a major turning point on the Eastern Front?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Stalingrad (1942-43) was a massive Soviet victory that halted the German advance and turned the tide on the Eastern Front.

questions.question: What was D-Day?
options: The Allied invasion of Normandy, France
questions.explanation: D-Day (June 6, 1944) was the Allied amphibious invasion of Normandy, France — one of the largest military operations in history.

questions.question: Approximately how many people died in the Holocaust?
questions.explanation: Approximately 6 million Jews were systematically murdered in the Holocaust, along with millions of other victims.

questions.question: Which international organisation was founded after WWII to maintain peace?
questions.explanation: The United Nations was founded in 1945 to replace the failed League of Nations and maintain international peace and security.
